skill.name: skill-share
skill.description: Create Claude skills, validate their structure, package them for distribution, and share them with a team through Slack using Rube.
skill.summary: Create, validate, package, and share Claude skills with team visibility.
skill.author: Joseph OBrien

security_audit.summary: The static weak cryptography blockers at SKILL.md lines 3, 55, and 60 are false positives from ordinary prose, with no cryptographic API or algorithm use present. The external command finding at SKILL.md lines 67-73 is also a false positive because the fenced block is a plain-text example, not executable shell or Ruby code. The skill describes creating directories and zip packages, so publication is low risk with normal user review of generated files and Slack destinations.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Packaging Workflow
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to create skill directories and package zip files. This is an intended capability, but users should review generated paths and archive contents before distribution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly describes directory creation and zip packaging. The behavior is legitimate for a skill creation workflow, but it affects the local filesystem.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptographic algorithm matches at SKILL.md lines 3, 55, and 60 are ordinary documentation text. No evidence found of weak cryptographic APIs, hashing functions, or encryption behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain prose about sharing, assets, and initialization. There is no code or cryptographic operation in the skill file.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution at SKILL.md lines 67-73 is a fenced plain-text example. It does not contain executable commands, interpolation, or shell syntax.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The location is a text code fence that explains a sample workflow. No command execution primitive appears in the cited lines.

content.value_statement: Teams can lose track of useful AI skills when creation and sharing are manual. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through skill creation, validation, packaging, and Slack sharing.
actual_capabilities: Creates structured skill directories with a SKILL.md file.
Generates standard scripts, references, and assets directories.
Adds YAML frontmatter with required skill metadata.
Checks naming conventions and metadata completeness.
Packages completed skills into distributable zip files.
Shares created skills on Slack channels using Rube.
limitations: The repository contains instructions only and no implementation scripts.
Slack sharing requires Rube access and configured Slack permissions.
Generated skills still need human review before team distribution.
It does not guarantee acceptance by any public marketplace.

use_cases.title: Standardize Internal Skill Creation
use_cases.description: Create team skills with consistent folders, metadata, validation steps, and package naming.

use_cases.title: Share New Skills With a Team
use_cases.description: Package a completed skill and announce it in Slack so teammates can discover and try it.

use_cases.title: Prepare Skills for Review
use_cases.description: Validate naming, required fields, and package contents before sending a skill to reviewers.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new Claude skill named meeting-summary. Include a clear description, required metadata, and standard folders.

prompt_templates.title: Validate a Skill Draft
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my skill folder for required SKILL.md fields, hyphen-case naming, and missing standard directories.

prompt_templates.title: Package and Announce a Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Package the customer-research skill for distribution and draft a Slack announcement for the product team.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Team Skill Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a repeatable workflow for authoring, validating, packaging, and sharing new Claude skills across our team.

output_examples.input: Create a skill named pdf-analyzer for extracting key points from PDFs.
output_examples.output: A new skill folder is prepared with metadata, standard directories, validation notes, and packaging guidance.

output_examples.input: Validate this skill before I share it with my team.
output: The required metadata fields are present.
The package is ready after reviewing the generated documentation.

output_examples.input: Share the finished onboarding-helper skill in Slack.
output_examples.output: A concise Slack announcement is drafted with the skill name, purpose, package location, and recommended next step.

best_practices: Review generated SKILL.md content before packaging or sharing.
Confirm the Slack channel and audience before sending announcements.
Keep skill names short, descriptive, and hyphen-case.
anti_patterns: Do not package secrets, credentials, or private data inside skill assets.
Do not share an unvalidated skill with a broad Slack channel.
Do not rely on generated metadata without checking accuracy.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates a structured Claude skill folder with SKILL.md metadata and standard supporting directories.

faq.question: Can it share skills on Slack?
faq.answer: Yes. The documentation describes Slack sharing through Rube, which requires the proper access and configuration.

faq.question: Does it include executable scripts?
faq.answer: No. The published file is an instruction document and does not include implementation scripts.

faq.question: Can I use it with Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: Does it validate skill structure?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes checks for SKILL.md format, required fields, naming conventions, and metadata completeness.

faq.question: Should generated packages be reviewed?
faq.answer: Yes. Review paths, package contents, and Slack announcements before distributing any generated skill.
